heading: Air Force nurses: Chief; appointment
label: 9064 Air Force nurses: Chief; appointment
notes: Amendments2019—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 116–92, § 952(b)(1)section 9069 of this titleSubsec. (a). , substituted “is” for “are”. Amendment was directed to and was executed before amendment by , see above, pursuant to , set out as a Coordination of Certain Sections of an Act With Other Provisions of That Act note under .Pub. L. 116–92, § 1731(a)(61)section 9069 of this titlesection 952(b)(1) of Pub. L. 116–92section 1731(f) of Pub. L. 116–92section 101 of this title2018— renumbered as .Pub. L. 115–232section 8069 of this titlesection 9069 of this title2016—, amended section catchline generally, substituting “Air Force nurses: Chief; appointment” for “Air Force nurses: Chief and assistant chief; appointment; grade”.Pub. L. 114–328, § 502(nn)(2)Subsec. (a). , substituted “Position of Chief” for “Positions of Chief and Assistant Chief” in heading and struck out “and assistant chief” after “There are a Chief” in text.Pub. L. 114–328, § 502(nn)(1)(A)Subsec. (b). , struck out second sentence which read as follows: “An appointee who holds a lower regular grade shall be appointed in the regular grade of major general.”Pub. L. 114–328, § 502(nn)(1)(B)Subsec. (c). , struck out subsec. (c). Text read as follows: “The Surgeon General shall appoint the assistant chief from the officers of the Regular Air Force designated as Air Force nurses whose regular grade is above lieutenant colonel.”Pub. L. 114–328, § 502(nn)(1)(C)2002—Subsec. (b). substituted “major general” for “brigadier general” in second sentence.Pub. L. 107–3141998—Subsec. (b). struck out “, but not for more than three years, and may not be reappointed to the same position” after “pleasure of the Secretary”.Pub. L. 105–261

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019

children.heading: .—Position of Chief
children.label: a .—Position of Chief
children.text: There is a Chief of the Air Force Nurse Corps.

children.heading: .—Chief
children.label: b .—Chief
children.text: The Secretary of the Air Force shall appoint the Chief from the officers of the Regular Air Force designated as Air Force nurses whose regular grade is above lieutenant colonel and who are recommended by the Surgeon General. The Chief serves during the pleasure of the Secretary.
